information: Added max_file_size field to ScanConfig in config.rs with a default of 10MB (10_485_760 bytes). The scanner checks file size against this limit after getting metadata but before computing blake3 hash, preventing OOM on huge files. Files exceeding the limit are skipped with a warning logged via eprintln! and tracked via the new files_size_skipped counter in ScanResult. The size check was placed after the files_scanned counter (which tracks all files that pass ignore filters) - this means files_size_skipped is a subset of files_scanned. Key implementation detail: check must use  greater) not  allow files exactly at the limit. Default serde serialization uses #[serde(default)] for seamless TOML config support.

Added configurable max_findings limit to the analyzer (default 500). In detect_all(), findings are now sorted by severity (Likely  and truncated to max_findings with a warning emitted via eprintln!. Added severity_rank() method to Verdict enum. Key learnings: (1) When testing sort-by-severity truncation, ensure each test block fires ONLY the intended detectors by carefully setting forwarded_params vs param_count combinations. (2) The log crate isn't set up in this project, so eprintln! is the right choice for warnings. (3) Verdict doesn't derive Ord, so a custom severity_rank() method was added instead of reordering variants.

Fixed CRITICAL bug in Finding::new(): The old ID used block.file_path.file_stem() + block.start_line which was unstable — IDs would change when lines shifted or when different files had the same stem. The fix uses block.id (blake3 hash of file_path+code) combined with pattern_name for a stable, unique identifier. Pattern: "pattern_name-block_id". Also updated lessons: when generating finding IDs, always use a content-hash (stable under renames and line shifts), never positional data like start_line.

Merged duplicate CallGraph types: the analyzer CallGraph (src/analyzer/call_graph.rs) became the canonical type with Serialize/Deserialize derives, and the cache CallGraph (src/cache.rs) was removed. The cache now imports CallGraph from the analyzer module. Key details:
- Added serde::{Serialize, Deserialize} to analyzer::CallGraph
- Removed the to_cache_call_graph() conversion method - it's no longer needed since both types are the same
- Changed the test from verifying conversion roundtrip to verifying bincode serde roundtrip
- Updated main.rs to use direct assignment (graph.clone()) instead of conversion
- Used #[serde(default)] on reverse_edges is no longer needed on the canonical type (it was on the cache one for backward-compat), but the canonical type doesn't need it since fresh builds will always have the field
- The HashMap import is still needed in cache.rs (used by MtimeIndex and AliasMap)

When adding reverse_edges to CallGraph, ALL manually constructed CallGraph instances in tests must also populate reverse_edges. The compute_indirection_depth function uses get_callers() which now relies on reverse_edges. Any test that manually inserts into forward edges without inserting into reverse_edges will break. Also, for cache serialization, new fields added to the cache::CallGraph struct must use #[serde(default)] for backward compatibility with existing cache files.

Implemented `forwarded_params` detection in the JS/TS parser (src/parser/javascript.rs). The helper function `count_forwarded_params` uses regex-based body scanning: it finds brace-delimited bodies via brace matching (or uses line-limited scanning for expression-body arrows), then matches `identifier(args)` patterns to detect when function parameters are passed directly to inner calls. Returns count of unique params forwarded. Imports HashSet for efficient dedup. Added 15 unit tests covering: all/some/none params forwarded, no params, arrow/async functions, multiple calls, nested bodies, expression args (not counted), TypeScript annotations, method calls, class (not computed), and empty bodies. All tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.

Before Week 3 "core patterns" development, did a comprehensive codebase audit. Fixed: (1) Hid speculative --deep flag with hide=true, (2) Removed dead code in resolve_alias_path (always-true conditional with unreachable slashed variable), (3) Deleted orphaned placeholder files (architectural.rs, identifier.rs) that weren't declared in any module, (4) Replaced 20+ .display() calls with .to_string_lossy() for robustness. Kept planned-for-Week-3 PatternType variants (SpeculativeGenerality, ArchitecturalSlop) and config fields (speculative_generality_variants, speculative_generality_used) since they're intentionally stubbed for Week 3. All 168 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean.
